Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Penalties Sought As Part Of A Settlement Pursuant To The Private Attorneys General Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STAN MALLISON & ATTY. ERIC TRABUCCO OF MARLLISON & MARTINEZ, TEL. NO. 1-510-832-9999 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL A. FARBSTEIN OF FARBSTEIN & BLACKMAN, TEL. NO. 1-650-554-6200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS CALLED TWICE ON THE RECORD FOR THE COURT TO REVIEW A LATE FILING SUBMITTED BY THE PLAINTIFF. THE ISSUES DISCUSSED ARE THE ADMINISTRATIVE COST, AMOUNT OF PENALTIES AND THE LOADSTAR REGARDING ATTORNEY'S FEE. THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF MEGAN TIEDMANN'S Motion To Approve Penalties Sought As Part Of A Settlement Pursuant To The Private Attorneys General Act is Granted. Order signed in open court. Moving party to prepare form of judgment. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189

Motion To Approve Paga Penalties; Memorandum Of Point And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-17-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MEGAN TIEDMANN's Motion To Approve PAGA Penalties claim is denied without prejudice to being refiled. The proof of service filed for the motion papers do not show that the LWDA was served with those papers as required by Labor Code 2699(l)(2). Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
